channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The next big AI shift in media? Turning news into a two-way conversation
item.description: When people talk about how AI might reshape media, the term “hyper-personalization” comes up a lot. In broad terms, it means that AI can tailor the experience around your preferences—assuming it has enough data about you.
item.dc:creator: Pete Pachal, Fast Company

item.title: Trump’s billionaire pick to lead NASA sparks debate over the future of space exploration
item.description: Jared Isaacman, billionaire, CEO and nominee to become the next NASA administrator, faced questions on April 9, 2025, from members of the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation during his confirmation hearing for the position.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: iPhones made in the USA? Here’s why that won’t happen, even with Trump’s tariffs on China
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has been predicting its barrage of tariffs targeting China will push Apple into manufacturing the iPhone in the United States for the first time. But that’s an unlikely scenario even with U.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Google lays off hundreds, impacting Android and Pixel teams
item.description: Alphabet’s Google laid off hundreds of employees in its platforms and devices unit, The Information reported on Friday, citing a person with direct knowledge of the situation.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Venmo’s dubious defaults look like a permanent privacy foul
item.description: If it weren’t for Signal, Venmo might be the most infamous app of the Trump administration—and maybe the most beloved among journalists covering this White House.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: Irish privacy watchdog investigates Elon Musk's X's use of personal data to train Grok AI chatbot
item.description: Ireland’s data privacy watchdog says it’s investigating Elon Musk’s social media platform X over its use of personal data to train his artificial intelligence chatbot Grok.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Colombia's capital ends drought-related water rationing. Its case is a warning to other cities
item.description: Colombia’s capital has always seemed immune to water scarcity, nestled among cloud-kissed Andean peaks and known for steady rainfall.
item.dc:creator: Astrid Suarez And Steven Grattan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Waymo robotaxis make up 20% of Uber rides in Austin, data shows
item.description: Waymo robotaxis made up about 20% of rides offered by Uber Technologies Inc. in Austin in the last week of March, underscoring how consumers have warmed to the autonomous service since the companies launched a partnership early last month.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Garmin has just crossed a powerful bike light with a 4K dash cam – and as a cyclist, I’m all in
item.description: Garmin has just announced a powerful front bike light that also doubles as a 4K camera for capturing run-ins with other road users.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: MSI has apparently leaked new its Nvidia RTX 5060 Ti third-party GPUs, and I’m worried these budget GPUs will come with a ‘new motherboard’ tax
item.description: MSI's third-party RTX 5060 Ti GPUs are on the horizon, with new images leaked revealing their PCIe 5.0 interface.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: WhatsApp has just dropped these 9 new features – including 2 that I'm not happy about
item.description: WhatsApp has just rolled out an endless list of new call and chat functions, but two of them have really rubbed users the wrong way.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Silicon Valley needs to get back to silicon
item.description: With their drab gray suits and their Buddy Holly glasses, the so-called traitorous eight don’t look like revolutionaries. Given no context, you can imagine them occupying some kind of middle-management role at a small regional bank. And yet these are the people you can thank for the digital world.
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo Aramburu, Fast Company

item.title: Sununu knocks Musk over 'bad messaging' about DOGE cuts
item.description: Former New Hampshire Gov. Chris Sununu (R) knocked Elon Musk for “bad messaging” about cuts ordered by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE). “I think the key here is bad messaging, right? I think people appreciate waste, fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Two tempting budget Bluetooth turntables just landed in the UK, in time for Record Store Day 2025
item.description: Majority launches two vinyl players for Record Store Day and every other day, and you can choose with or without speakers
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Teenager Seizure-Free After Life-Changing Brain Implant Technology
item.description: A 17-year-old girl's life was transformed with a groundbreaking brain implant technology that made her seizure-free for the first time in years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Waymo gets stuck in California Chick-fil-A drive-thru
item.description: Videos showed Chick-fil-A workers standing around the stalled vehicle, which had its hazard lights on.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: Microsoft says it's 'slowing or pausing' some AI data center projects, including $1B plan for Ohio
item.description: Microsoft said it is “slowing or pausing” some of its data center construction, including a $1 billion project in Ohio, the latest sign that the demand for artificial intelligence technology that drove a massive infrastructure expansion might not need quite as many powerful computers as expected.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Scientists genetically engineer wolves with white hair and muscular jaws like the extinct dire wolf
item.description: Three genetically engineered wolves that may resemble extinct dire wolves are trotting, sleeping and howling in an undisclosed secure location in the U.S., according to the company that aims to bring back lost species.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA LARSON, Associated Press, KRON4
